category_title: Disengaged adolescent with behavioral history and word-skipping habits
minimal_context: Derek is reading a page of text independently during a general English class assignment.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please read this page of the text for your independent reading assignment.

STUDENT: (Scowls) Okay. (Reads the page as quickly as possible)

TUTOR: What did you understand from that page?

STUDENT: (Speaks quickly in short bursts) Nothing. It's boring.

TUTOR: Go back and try to make sense of the words on the page.

STUDENT: I'm not rereading it, I just want to finish the task. My eyes just seem to skip over words as I’m going down the page.

options.A: Place a hand on the student's shoulder to provide a physical prompt during the reading task.
options.B: Ignore all of the undesirable behaviors exhibited by the student during the independent reading assignment.
options.C: Provide enthusiastic praise to the student for finishing the page quickly and encourage continued reading.
options.D: Model how to read and learn the text through teaching strategies in short time segments.
